sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Topics 67

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Topics 67:1
items.html: (to the question) For at the time of the breaking of the vessels, when the Lights departed from within the Kelim [vessels], they left behind them their Reshimot [records] in Atzilut, but the bodies of the Kelim and the sparks in them fell into BYA [Beria, Yetzira, Assiya]. Therefore, at the time of their correction, the essential Zivug [coupling] is made upon the Reshimot, which are the Lights, and these raise to themselves the Kelim and the sparks, according to the measure of the level of the Zivug of the Mayin Duchrin [male waters].
